error.signature: AI tells a traveler that China always requires a visa arranged at an embassy in advance

verdict.summary: China has expanded entry options. A unilateral visa-free policy now lets ordinary passport holders of many countries enter for up to 30 days for tourism or business without a visa (the eligible list changed through 2024-2025), and visa-free transit was extended to 240 hours (10 days) at many ports. The lists update frequently, so verify the current rules.

dead_ends.action: Assume every traveler to China must obtain a consular visa in advance
dead_ends.why_fails: China introduced unilateral visa-free entry (up to 30 days) for many nationalities and 240-hour visa-free transit; assuming a consular visa is always required is outdated for eligible travelers.
dead_ends.common_misconception: The unilateral visa-free and expanded transit policies are recent and frequently overlooked.

dead_ends.action: Rely on visa-free transit while leaving the permitted area or exceeding the time limit
dead_ends.why_fails: Visa-free transit (e.g., 240 hours) is limited to specific ports/regions and requires an onward ticket to a third country or region; overstaying or leaving the permitted zone breaches it.

workarounds.action: Check the current unilateral visa-free list and conditions on the National Immigration Administration site; if eligible, enter visa-free for up to 30 days.
workarounds.how: Verify your nationality and purpose on nia.gov.cn before relying on visa-free entry.

workarounds.action: For transit, confirm the port supports 240-hour visa-free and carry an onward ticket to a third country/region.
workarounds.how: Check port eligibility and keep proof of onward travel for transit visa-free.
